Basic abbreviation
.
*[CSS]: Cascading Style Sheets

This is some CSS here.
.
<p>This is some <abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr> here.</p>
.

Multiple abbreviations
.
*[abbr]: Abbreviations
*[CSS]: Cascading Style Sheets

This abbr uses CSS.
.
<p>This <abbr title="Abbreviations">abbr</abbr> uses <abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr>.</p>
.

Word boundary - no match inside word
.
*[CSS]: Cascading Style Sheets

NoCSS CSSNo xCSSx CSS
.
<p>NoCSS CSSNo xCSSx <abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr></p>
.

Abbreviation at start of text
.
*[CSS]: Cascading Style Sheets

CSS at start.
.
<p><abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr> at start.</p>
.

Abbreviation at end of text
.
*[CSS]: Cascading Style Sheets

At end CSS
.
<p>At end <abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr></p>
.

Multiple occurrences
.
*[CSS]: Cascading Style Sheets

CSS and CSS and CSS
.
<p><abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr> and <abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr> and <abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr></p>
.

Definition at end of document
.
CSS here.

*[CSS]: Cascading Style Sheets
.
<p><abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr> here.</p>
.

Abbreviation in bold
.
*[CSS]: Cascading Style Sheets

This **CSS** is bold.
.
<p>This <strong><abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr></strong> is bold.</p>
.

Abbreviation in italic
.
*[CSS]: Cascading Style Sheets

This *CSS* is italic.
.
<p>This <em><abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets">CSS</abbr></em> is italic.</p>
.

Abbreviation in heading
.
*[CSS]: Cascading

# CSS heading
.
<h1><abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr> heading</h1>
.

Abbreviation in link text
.
*[CSS]: Cascading

[CSS](http://example.com)
.
<p><a href="http://example.com"><abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr></a></p>
.

Abbreviation in list
.
*[CSS]: Cascading

- list with CSS
.
<ul>
<li>list with <abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr></li>
</ul>
.

Abbreviation in blockquote
.
*[CSS]: Cascading

> quote with CSS
.
<blockquote>
<p>quote with <abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr></p>
</blockquote>
.

Multiple paragraphs
.
*[CSS]: Cascading

First CSS.

Second CSS.
.
<p>First <abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr>.</p>
<p>Second <abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr>.</p>
.

HTML chars in title
.
*[CSS]: Cascading <Style> & "Sheets"

The CSS here.
.
<p>The <abbr title="Cascading &lt;Style&gt; &amp; &quot;Sheets&quot;">CSS</abbr> here.</p>
.

Empty title is invalid
.
*[CSS]:

The CSS here.
.
<p>*[CSS]:</p>
<p>The CSS here.</p>
.

Spaces-only title is invalid
.
*[CSS]:  

The CSS here.
.
<p>*[CSS]:</p>
<p>The CSS here.</p>
.

Single char abbreviation
.
*[C]: Charlie

The letter C here.
.
<p>The letter <abbr title="Charlie">C</abbr> here.</p>
.

Abbreviation with numbers
.
*[CSS3]: Cascading Style Sheets 3

CSS3 is modern.
.
<p><abbr title="Cascading Style Sheets 3">CSS3</abbr> is modern.</p>
.

Abbreviation next to punctuation
.
*[CSS]: Cascading

CSS, CSS. CSS! CSS? (CSS) [CSS]
.
<p><abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr>, <abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr>. <abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr>! <abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr>? (<abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr>) [<abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr>]</p>
.

Definition between paragraphs
.
First paragraph.

*[CSS]: Cascading

Second CSS.
.
<p>First paragraph.</p>
<p>Second <abbr title="Cascading">CSS</abbr>.</p>
.

Abbreviation with typographer enabled
.
*[HTML]: Hyper Text Markup Language

The HTML spec is "great" -- really (c).
.
<p>The <abbr title="Hyper Text Markup Language">HTML</abbr> spec is &quot;great&quot; -- really (c).</p>
.

Abbreviation with multiple entries and no matches
.
*[CSS]: Cascading Style Sheets
*[HTML]: Hyper Text Markup Language

No abbreviations here.
.
<p>No abbreviations here.</p>
.
